title: Mexico â€“ Semarnat Authorizes a $1.3 Billion Green Hydrogen Project, the First of Its Kind in Sinaloa
content:
Mexicoâ€™s Ministry of Environment and Natural Resources (Semarnat) has granted environmental approval for the countryâ€™s first large-scale green hydrogen project in Sinaloa, marking a milestone for Latin Americaâ€™s clean-energy transition. Known as the Tango Solar Norte project, it represents an estimated US $1.3 billion investment combining a vast photovoltaic (solar) plant with electrolyzers to produce green hydrogen. The project will generate renewable electricity to split water into hydrogen and oxygen, producing zero-carbon hydrogen for industrial and transportation uses.

Located in northern Sinaloa, Tango Solar Norte is expected to produce green hydrogen at commercial scale and catalyze the development of related infrastructure such as pipelines and storage. It also aligns with Mexicoâ€™s efforts to draft its first national hydrogen strategy, aiming to diversify energy sources and cut greenhouse gas emissions. Supporters highlight its potential to create jobs, attract investment, and help decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors, while environmental oversight by Semarnat seeks to ensure sustainable development and minimal ecological impact.
author: Analysts Account
